name: Long Horizon Grid Balancer
description: Act as a Grid Operator managing a battery reserve against a hidden evening demand spike. Strategically spend a budget on forecasts of varying precision to locate the peak before committing to a final 24-hour dispatch schedule.

binding_vow.description: Each turn, either buy a forecast of the hidden net load at one hour (cost vs. noise trade-off across 4 tiers) or submit a final 24-hour battery dispatch schedule. Score = 1000 if the dispatch prevents grid failure at the true hidden evening peak, else 0, minus total forecast cost. A good agent buys just enough precision to pin down the peak hour and magnitude, then concentrates its limited battery reserve there.

observation_space.description: { role, horizon_hours:24, grid_capacity, battery_reserve, max_discharge_rate, evening_window:[lo,hi], forecast_tiers:{tier:{cost,noise_std}}, budget_spent, steps_taken, max_steps, forecasts_purchased:[{hour,tier,observed}], last_forecast, action_schema }

reward.description: Forecast steps reward 0. Final dispatch (or timeout) yields (1000 if grid failure prevented at the true peak else 0) - total_cost_of_forecasts.
